questions.question_text: How many hours of pre-licensing education must a DC salesperson candidate complete?
distractors: 40 hours
90 hours
135 hours
questions.explanation: DC requires 60 hours of approved pre-licensing education for salesperson candidates. The 135-hour requirement applies to broker candidates.

questions.question_text: A 17-year-old completes the 60-hour pre-licensing course and passes the exam. Can they obtain a DC salesperson license?
questions.correct_answer: No, they must be at least 18 years old
distractors: Yes, if they have a sponsoring broker
Yes, with parental consent required
No, the minimum age is 21 in DC
questions.explanation: DC requires salesperson candidates to be at least 18 years old. Passing the exam and completing education alone are not sufficient if the age requirement is not met.

questions.question_text: What passing score is required on each portion of the DC real estate licensing exam?
questions.explanation: Candidates must score at least 75% on both the national and state portions of the PSI-administered exam. The two portions are scored separately.

questions.question_text: A candidate passes the national portion but fails the state portion of the DC exam. What happens next?
questions.correct_answer: They must retake the state portion within 6 months
distractors: They must retake both portions immediately
They keep the national score for 12 months
They must complete additional coursework first
questions.explanation: If a candidate fails one portion, they must retake it within 6 months. If that window expires without passing, both portions must be retaken.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is required as part of the DC salesperson licensing process?
distractors: Two years of real estate experience
Completion of a post-licensing course
Passing a separate ethics examination
questions.explanation: DC requires a background check including fingerprinting for all salesperson license applicants. There is no experience requirement for the salesperson license or separate post-licensing course mandate.

questions.question_text: Before a DC salesperson can receive their license, they must be affiliated with which type of licensee?
distractors: Any licensed salesperson
A licensed property manager
A licensed DC attorney
questions.explanation: DC salespersons must be sponsored by a licensed DC broker. Salespersons cannot operate independently and must work under broker supervision.

questions.question_text: What distinguishes a DC broker license from a salesperson license?
questions.correct_answer: Brokers can operate firms and hold trust accounts
distractors: Brokers only need 60 hours of education
Salespersons can supervise other licensees
Brokers do not need continuing education
questions.explanation: DC brokers can operate their own firms, supervise salespersons, and hold trust accounts. Salespersons must work under a broker and cannot perform these functions independently.

questions.question_text: A salesperson wants to become a broker in DC. How many hours of total education are required for a broker license?
distractors: 60 hours
90 hours
180 hours
questions.explanation: DC requires 135 hours of approved education for a broker license. This includes the salesperson coursework plus additional broker-level courses.

questions.question_text: A DC salesperson's sponsoring broker retires and closes their firm. What must the salesperson do to continue practicing?
questions.correct_answer: Affiliate with a new licensed broker
distractors: Apply for a temporary independent license
Wait 90 days before working with clients
Automatically receive a broker upgrade
questions.explanation: Salespersons cannot practice without broker sponsorship. When a broker closes their firm, the salesperson must find and affiliate with a new licensed broker to continue practicing.

questions.question_text: How many years of active licensure as a salesperson does DC require before a candidate can apply for a broker license?
distractors: At least 1 year
At least 3 years
At least 5 years
questions.explanation: DC requires broker candidates to have been actively licensed as a salesperson for at least 2 years (or equivalent experience) in addition to completing 135 hours of education.

questions.question_text: Which of the following activities requires a DC real estate license?
questions.correct_answer: Negotiating a property sale for compensation
distractors: Selling your own personal residence
Managing your own rental property
Providing legal advice on a real estate matter
questions.explanation: Negotiating, listing, selling, or managing real property for others for compensation requires a DC real estate license. Selling your own property, managing your own rentals, and providing legal advice (attorney) do not.

questions.question_text: A friend asks you to help find a buyer for their DC condo in exchange for a referral fee. You do not hold a real estate license. Is this legal?
questions.correct_answer: No, brokerage for compensation requires a license
distractors: Yes, referral fees do not require a license
Yes, if the referral amount is under $1,000
Yes, as long as no written contract is signed
questions.explanation: Performing real estate brokerage activities for compensation, including referring buyers or sellers for a fee, requires a DC real estate license. The amount of compensation is irrelevant.

questions.question_text: Which person is exempt from DC real estate licensing requirements?
questions.correct_answer: An owner selling their own property
distractors: A paid property locator for tenants
A salesperson working without a broker
An unlicensed assistant showing properties
questions.explanation: Owners selling or leasing their own property are generally exempt from licensing. Paid property locators, salespersons (who must have broker sponsorship), and unlicensed assistants performing licensed activities all need licensing or supervision.

questions.question_text: Auctioning real property in DC on behalf of another person for compensation requires what?
distractors: Only an auctioneer permit
No license if the sale is court-ordered
Only a business license from DLCP
questions.explanation: Auctioning real property on behalf of others for compensation is a licensed real estate activity in DC, requiring the auctioneer to hold a valid real estate license.

questions.question_text: A DC property management company leases apartments and collects rent for building owners. Does this require real estate licensing?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, managing for others for pay requires a license
distractors: No, property management is fully exempt
Only if the building has 10 or more units
Only if the company charges a commission
questions.explanation: Property management activities performed for others for compensation, including leasing and rent collection, require a real estate license in DC. The size of the building and fee structure are irrelevant.
